rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH K. BRAVO VS. GLORIA POUNCIL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Confirm Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 22, 2019, Line 1, PETITIONER JOSEPH BRAVO's Petition To Confirm Award. Petitioner Joseph Bravo's petition to confirm arbitration award is granted. The award of $401,760 is confirmed. The court denies Mr. Bravo's request for $3,500. Page 8 of the award indicates that sum is Mr. Bravo's debt and there is no adduced basis to shift the debt to Ms. Pouncil. Mr. Bravo may recover $450 for filing the instant petition to confirm arbitration award pursuant to Business & Professions Code § 6203(c). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Mr. Bravo is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portions of the tentative ruling and attaches a copy of the arbitration award and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: WISHING WELL FARMS VS. FARMGIRL FLOWERS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 22, 2019, Line 2, PETITIONER WISHING WELL FARMS' Motion To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award Off calendar. The court did not receive courtesy copies of the filings as required by SF Local Rule 2.7B. In addition, the court's register of actions does not show that petitioner served both the underlying petition and the notice of motion. The only document that the court can determine was served is the request for court call appearance. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SAMANTHA RUSK VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Enforcing Terms Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 22, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's Motion For Order Enforcing Terms Of Settlement Agreement. Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to enforce settlement pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 664.6 is denied. Ford fails to present a settlement that complies with the direct party participation and retained jurisdiction requirements of § 664.6. A Code of Civil Procedure § 998 settlement is not the equivalent of a § 664.6 settlement. In addition, the present dispute is really between Plaintiff's former and present counsel, not Plaintiff and Defendant. Ford, Plaintiff, California Lemon Law Group ("CLLG"), and the Knight Law Group are ordered to meet and confer immediately to resolve this issue. If they cannot come to a resolution, then Ford may need to interplead the $25,000 pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 386 and may be entitled to recover its fees for having to commence such an action from the $25,000. (See City of Morgan Hill v. Brown (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1114.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: SUSANAH MOVSESSIAN VS. GAP INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Gap, Inc.S Further Responses And Production To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday , April 22, 2019, Line 2, PLAINTIFF SUSANAH MOVSESSIAN's Motion To Compel Defendant Gap, Inc.S Further Responses And Production To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN MOORE VS. DICK'S SPORTING GOODS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And [Proposed] Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 22, 2019, Line 10, PLAINTIFF JOHN MOORE's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And [Proposed] Consent Judgment Plaintiff John Moore's motion to approve Proposition 65 settlement is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The court will execute the proposed order and proposed judgment included with the moving papers. =(302/EPS)
